<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 900 620" font-family="-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="billed" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="0%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#6366f1"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#8b5cf6"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <marker id="arrowhead" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="8" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto">
      <path d="M2 1L8 5L2 9" fill="none" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <!-- background -->
  <rect width="900" height="620" fill="#fafafa"/>

  <!-- title -->
  <text x="40" y="46" font-size="24" font-weight="700" fill="#1e293b">01 · Business mechanics — the money flow</text>
  <text x="40" y="72" font-size="14" fill="#64748b">A single $200 line item, decomposed. The order of operations is the entire game.</text>

  <!-- billed bar -->
  <rect x="40" y="110" width="820" height="56" rx="8" fill="url(#billed)"/>
  <text x="450" y="138" font-size="18" font-weight="600" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">$200 billed by provider</text>
  <text x="450" y="158" font-size="12" fill="#e0e7ff" text-anchor="middle">PHYSIO service, single visit</text>

  <!-- divider arrow -->
  <line x1="450" y1="175" x2="450" y2="200" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arrowhead)"/>

  <!-- decomposition row -->
  <g>
    <!-- deductible -->
    <rect x="40" y="210" width="200" height="80" rx="8" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <text x="140" y="234" font-size="12" font-weight="600" fill="#92400e" text-anchor="middle">DEDUCTIBLE_APPLIED</text>
    <text x="140" y="262" font-size="28" font-weight="700" fill="#1e293b" text-anchor="middle">$50</text>
    <text x="140" y="282" font-size="11" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">member pays first</text>

    <!-- copay -->
    <rect x="252" y="210" width="200" height="80" rx="8" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <text x="352" y="234" font-size="12" font-weight="600" fill="#92400e" text-anchor="middle">COPAY_APPLIED</text>
    <text x="352" y="262" font-size="28" font-weight="700" fill="#1e293b" text-anchor="middle">$20</text>
    <text x="352" y="282" font-size="11" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">fixed per visit</text>

    <!-- coinsurance -->
    <rect x="464" y="210" width="200" height="80" rx="8" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <text x="564" y="234" font-size="12" font-weight="600" fill="#92400e" text-anchor="middle">COINSURANCE_APPLIED</text>
    <text x="564" y="262" font-size="28" font-weight="700" fill="#1e293b" text-anchor="middle">$26</text>
    <text x="564" y="282" font-size="11" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">20% × $130 remainder</text>

    <!-- payable -->
    <rect x="676" y="210" width="184" height="80" rx="8" fill="#ccfbf1" stroke="#14b8a6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <text x="768" y="234" font-size="12" font-weight="600" fill="#0f766e" text-anchor="middle">APPROVED · payable</text>
    <text x="768" y="262" font-size="28" font-weight="700" fill="#0f766e" text-anchor="middle">$104</text>
    <text x="768" y="282" font-size="11" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">insurer pays</text>
  </g>

  <!-- accumulator section -->
  <g transform="translate(0, 320)">
    <text x="40" y="0" font-size="16" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">Then the accumulator math</text>
    <rect x="40" y="14" width="820" height="86" rx="8" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#e2e8f0" stroke-width="1.5"/>

    <text x="60" y="46" font-size="13" fill="#64748b">Before this claim</text>
    <text x="60" y="68" font-size="22" font-weight="700" fill="#1e293b">$1,396</text>
    <text x="60" y="86" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">used against $1,500 PHYSIO annual limit</text>

    <line x1="240" y1="40" x2="240" y2="88" stroke="#e2e8f0" stroke-width="1"/>

    <text x="260" y="46" font-size="13" fill="#64748b">This payable</text>
    <text x="260" y="68" font-size="22" font-weight="700" fill="#1e293b">+ $104</text>
    <text x="260" y="86" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">would push total to $1,500</text>

    <line x1="440" y1="40" x2="440" y2="88" stroke="#e2e8f0" stroke-width="1"/>

    <text x="460" y="46" font-size="13" fill="#64748b">Cap at limit</text>
    <text x="460" y="68" font-size="22" font-weight="700" fill="#14b8a6">$104</text>
    <text x="460" y="86" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">fits exactly · no straddle</text>

    <line x1="620" y1="40" x2="620" y2="88" stroke="#e2e8f0" stroke-width="1"/>

    <text x="640" y="46" font-size="13" fill="#64748b">After writeback</text>
    <text x="640" y="68" font-size="22" font-weight="700" fill="#dc2626">$1,500</text>
    <text x="640" y="86" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">limit reached · next claim denies</text>
  </g>

  <!-- the EOB -->
  <g transform="translate(0, 450)">
    <text x="40" y="0" font-size="16" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">The Explanation of Benefits the member sees</text>
    <rect x="40" y="14" width="820" height="124" rx="8" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 4"/>
    <text x="60" y="42" font-size="13" font-family="monospace" fill="#1e293b">$200.00  billed by provider for PHYSIO</text>
    <text x="60" y="64" font-size="13" font-family="monospace" fill="#92400e">- $50.00  applied to your annual deductible (you still owe $0 of $500)</text>
    <text x="60" y="86" font-size="13" font-family="monospace" fill="#92400e">- $20.00  copay for this visit (you paid this at check-in)</text>
    <text x="60" y="108" font-size="13" font-family="monospace" fill="#92400e">- $26.00  your 20% coinsurance on the remainder</text>
    <text x="60" y="130" font-size="13" font-family="monospace" font-weight="700" fill="#0f766e">= $104.00 paid by your insurer · APPROVED</text>
  </g>

  <!-- footer caption -->
  <text x="450" y="600" font-size="12" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle" font-style="italic">Adjudication is deterministic. Reorder these four steps and every dollar downstream is wrong.</text>
</svg>

content.content:
1	<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 900 900" font-family="-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif">
2	  <defs>
3	    <marker id="ar3" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="8" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto">
4	      <path d="M2 1L8 5L2 9" fill="none" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
5	    </marker>
6	    <marker id="ar3-red" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="8" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto">
7	      <path d="M2 1L8 5L2 9" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/>
8	    </marker>
9	  </defs>
10	
11	  <rect width="900" height="900" fill="#fafafa"/>
12	
13	  <!-- title -->
14	  <text x="40" y="46" font-size="24" font-weight="700" fill="#1e293b">03 · The 11-step adjudication pipeline</text>
15	  <text x="40" y="72" font-size="14" fill="#64748b">Pure functions, in this exact order. Each step can short-circuit with a reason. Order is not negotiable.</text>
16	
17	  <!-- input -->
18	  <g>
19	    <rect x="280" y="100" width="340" height="60" rx="8" fill="#6366f1"/>
20	    <text x="450" y="124" font-size="14" font-weight="600" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">INPUT</text>
21	    <text x="450" y="146" font-size="12" font-family="monospace" fill="#e0e7ff" text-anchor="middle">(line_item, policy, accumulator)</text>
22	  </g>
23	
24	  <!-- steps -->
25	  <g font-family="-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif">
26	    <!-- 01 -->
27	    <rect x="40" y="190" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
28	    <circle cx="68" cy="214" r="16" fill="#6366f1"/>
29	    <text x="68" y="219" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">01</text>
30	    <text x="96" y="208" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">isPolicyActive(claim.date, policy.period)</text>
31	    <text x="96" y="226" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">check if policy is in force on the claim date</text>
32	    <rect x="560" y="200" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#fee2e2"/>
33	    <text x="710" y="219" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#991b1b" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">→ POLICY_NOT_ACTIVE · payable=0</text>
34	
35	    <!-- 02 -->
36	    <rect x="40" y="246" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
37	    <circle cx="68" cy="270" r="16" fill="#6366f1"/>
38	    <text x="68" y="275" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">02</text>
39	    <text x="96" y="264" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">findRule(line_item.service_type, policy.rules)</text>
40	    <text x="96" y="282" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">match the service to a coverage rule</text>
41	    <rect x="560" y="256" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#fee2e2"/>
42	    <text x="710" y="275" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#991b1b" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">→ NO_COVERAGE · payable=0</text>
43	
44	    <!-- 03 -->
45	    <rect x="40" y="302" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
46	    <circle cx="68" cy="326" r="16" fill="#6366f1"/>
47	    <text x="68" y="331" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">03</text>
48	    <text x="96" y="320" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">checkCovered(rule.covered)</text>
49	    <text x="96" y="338" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">explicit exclusion check</text>
50	    <rect x="560" y="312" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#fee2e2"/>
51	    <text x="710" y="331" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#991b1b" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">→ EXCLUDED · payable=0</text>
52	
53	    <!-- 04 -->
54	    <rect x="40" y="358" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
55	    <circle cx="68" cy="382" r="16" fill="#6366f1"/>
56	    <text x="68" y="387" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">04</text>
57	    <text x="96" y="376" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">checkPriorAuth(rule, line_item.prior_auth_id)</text>
58	    <text x="96" y="394" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">gate on authorization presence</text>
59	    <rect x="560" y="368" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#fef3c7"/>
60	    <text x="710" y="387" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#92400e" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">→ PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED · NEEDS_REVIEW</text>
61	
62	    <!-- 05 -->
63	    <rect x="40" y="414" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
64	    <circle cx="68" cy="438" r="16" fill="#6366f1"/>
65	    <text x="68" y="443" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">05</text>
66	    <text x="96" y="432" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">checkLimit(accumulator, rule.limit)</text>
67	    <text x="96" y="450" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">read accumulator, detect limit-reached or straddle</text>
68	    <rect x="560" y="424" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#fee2e2"/>
69	    <text x="710" y="443" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#991b1b" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">→ LIMIT_EXCEEDED · payable=0 or partial</text>
70	
71	    <!-- divider -->
72	    <line x1="40" y1="480" x2="860" y2="480" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-dasharray="3 3"/>
73	    <text x="450" y="500" font-size="11" font-weight="700" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="1">↓  IF WE GET HERE, THE LINE WILL PAY SOMETHING — NOW WE COMPUTE THE SPLIT  ↓</text>
74	
75	    <!-- 06 -->
76	    <rect x="40" y="514" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#14b8a6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
77	    <circle cx="68" cy="538" r="16" fill="#14b8a6"/>
78	    <text x="68" y="543" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">06</text>
79	    <text x="96" y="532" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">computeBasePayable(line_item, rule)</text>
80	    <text x="96" y="550" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">billed × units, capped at (limit − accumulator)</text>
81	    <rect x="560" y="524" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#e0e7ff"/>
82	    <text x="710" y="543" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#3730a3" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">base = $200</text>
83	
84	    <!-- 07 -->
85	    <rect x="40" y="570" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#14b8a6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
86	    <circle cx="68" cy="594" r="16" fill="#14b8a6"/>
87	    <text x="68" y="599" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">07</text>
88	    <text x="96" y="588" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">applyDeductible(base, policy.deductible_remaining)</text>
89	    <text x="96" y="606" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">subtract member's deductible share</text>
90	    <rect x="560" y="580" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#e0e7ff"/>
91	    <text x="710" y="599" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#3730a3" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">+ DEDUCTIBLE_APPLIED ($50)</text>
92	
93	    <!-- 08 -->
94	    <rect x="40" y="626" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#14b8a6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
95	    <circle cx="68" cy="650" r="16" fill="#14b8a6"/>
96	    <text x="68" y="655" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">08</text>
97	    <text x="96" y="644" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">applyCopay(base, rule.copay_cents)</text>
98	    <text x="96" y="662" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">subtract fixed copay</text>
99	    <rect x="560" y="636" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#e0e7ff"/>
100	    <text x="710" y="655" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#3730a3" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">+ COPAY_APPLIED ($20)</text>
101	
102	    <!-- 09 -->
103	    <rect x="40" y="682" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#14b8a6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
104	    <circle cx="68" cy="706" r="16" fill="#14b8a6"/>
105	    <text x="68" y="711" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">09</text>
106	    <text x="96" y="700" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">applyCoinsurance(base, rule.coinsurance_pct)</text>
107	    <text x="96" y="718" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">subtract percentage of the remainder</text>
108	    <rect x="560" y="692" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#e0e7ff"/>
109	    <text x="710" y="711" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#3730a3" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">+ COINSURANCE_APPLIED ($26)</text>
110	
111	    <!-- 10 -->
112	    <rect x="40" y="738" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#14b8a6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
113	    <circle cx="68" cy="762" r="16" fill="#14b8a6"/>
114	    <text x="68" y="767" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">10</text>
115	    <text x="96" y="756" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">capAtOopMax(policy.oop_max, accumulator.member_oop)</text>
116	    <text x="96" y="774" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">if member would exceed OOP max, insurer pays 100% from here</text>
117	    <rect x="560" y="748" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#e0e7ff"/>
118	    <text x="710" y="767" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#3730a3" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">+ OOP_MAX_REACHED (if applicable)</text>
119	
120	    <!-- 11 -->
121	    <rect x="40" y="794" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#14b8a6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
122	    <circle cx="68" cy="818" r="16" fill="#14b8a6"/>
123	    <text x="68" y="823" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">11</text>
124	    <text x="96" y="812" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">writeAccumulator(accumulator, payable)</text>
125	    <text x="96" y="830" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">single atomic write — same step every time</text>
126	    <rect x="560" y="804" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#ccfbf1"/>
127	    <text x="710" y="823" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#0f766e" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">= APPROVED · payable=$104</text>
128	  </g>
129	
130	  <!-- footer -->
131	  <text x="40" y="876" font-size="12" fill="#64748b" font-style="italic">Steps 1-5 can short-circuit. Steps 6-11 are arithmetic. Each step is a pure function. Each reason code has one test.</text>
132	</svg>
133

file.content:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 900 900" font-family="-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif">
  <defs>
    <marker id="ar3" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="8" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto">
      <path d="M2 1L8 5L2 9" fill="none" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    </marker>
    <marker id="ar3-red" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="8" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto">
      <path d="M2 1L8 5L2 9" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <rect width="900" height="900" fill="#fafafa"/>

  <!-- title -->
  <text x="40" y="46" font-size="24" font-weight="700" fill="#1e293b">03 · The 11-step adjudication pipeline</text>
  <text x="40" y="72" font-size="14" fill="#64748b">Pure functions, in this exact order. Each step can short-circuit with a reason. Order is not negotiable.</text>

  <!-- input -->
  <g>
    <rect x="280" y="100" width="340" height="60" rx="8" fill="#6366f1"/>
    <text x="450" y="124" font-size="14" font-weight="600" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">INPUT</text>
    <text x="450" y="146" font-size="12" font-family="monospace" fill="#e0e7ff" text-anchor="middle">(line_item, policy, accumulator)</text>
  </g>

  <!-- steps -->
  <g font-family="-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif">
    <!-- 01 -->
    <rect x="40" y="190" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="68" cy="214" r="16" fill="#6366f1"/>
    <text x="68" y="219" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">01</text>
    <text x="96" y="208" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">isPolicyActive(claim.date, policy.period)</text>
    <text x="96" y="226" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">check if policy is in force on the claim date</text>
    <rect x="560" y="200" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#fee2e2"/>
    <text x="710" y="219" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#991b1b" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">→ POLICY_NOT_ACTIVE · payable=0</text>

    <!-- 02 -->
    <rect x="40" y="246" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="68" cy="270" r="16" fill="#6366f1"/>
    <text x="68" y="275" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">02</text>
    <text x="96" y="264" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">findRule(line_item.service_type, policy.rules)</text>
    <text x="96" y="282" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">match the service to a coverage rule</text>
    <rect x="560" y="256" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#fee2e2"/>
    <text x="710" y="275" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#991b1b" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">→ NO_COVERAGE · payable=0</text>

    <!-- 03 -->
    <rect x="40" y="302" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="68" cy="326" r="16" fill="#6366f1"/>
    <text x="68" y="331" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">03</text>
    <text x="96" y="320" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">checkCovered(rule.covered)</text>
    <text x="96" y="338" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">explicit exclusion check</text>
    <rect x="560" y="312" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#fee2e2"/>
    <text x="710" y="331" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#991b1b" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">→ EXCLUDED · payable=0</text>

    <!-- 04 -->
    <rect x="40" y="358" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="68" cy="382" r="16" fill="#6366f1"/>
    <text x="68" y="387" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">04</text>
    <text x="96" y="376" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">checkPriorAuth(rule, line_item.prior_auth_id)</text>
    <text x="96" y="394" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">gate on authorization presence</text>
    <rect x="560" y="368" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#fef3c7"/>
    <text x="710" y="387" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#92400e" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">→ PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED · NEEDS_REVIEW</text>

    <!-- 05 -->
    <rect x="40" y="414" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="68" cy="438" r="16" fill="#6366f1"/>
    <text x="68" y="443" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">05</text>
    <text x="96" y="432" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">checkLimit(accumulator, rule.limit)</text>
    <text x="96" y="450" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">read accumulator, detect limit-reached or straddle</text>
    <rect x="560" y="424" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#fee2e2"/>
    <text x="710" y="443" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#991b1b" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">→ LIMIT_EXCEEDED · payable=0 or partial</text>

    <!-- divider -->
    <line x1="40" y1="480" x2="860" y2="480" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-dasharray="3 3"/>
    <text x="450" y="500" font-size="11" font-weight="700" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="1">↓  IF WE GET HERE, THE LINE WILL PAY SOMETHING — NOW WE COMPUTE THE SPLIT  ↓</text>

    <!-- 06 -->
    <rect x="40" y="514" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#14b8a6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="68" cy="538" r="16" fill="#14b8a6"/>
    <text x="68" y="543" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">06</text>
    <text x="96" y="532" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">computeBasePayable(line_item, rule)</text>
    <text x="96" y="550" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">billed × units, capped at (limit − accumulator)</text>
    <rect x="560" y="524" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#e0e7ff"/>
    <text x="710" y="543" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#3730a3" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">base = $200</text>

    <!-- 07 -->
    <rect x="40" y="570" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#14b8a6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="68" cy="594" r="16" fill="#14b8a6"/>
    <text x="68" y="599" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">07</text>
    <text x="96" y="588" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">applyDeductible(base, policy.deductible_remaining)</text>
    <text x="96" y="606" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">subtract member's deductible share</text>
    <rect x="560" y="580" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#e0e7ff"/>
    <text x="710" y="599" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#3730a3" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">+ DEDUCTIBLE_APPLIED ($50)</text>

    <!-- 08 -->
    <rect x="40" y="626" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#14b8a6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="68" cy="650" r="16" fill="#14b8a6"/>
    <text x="68" y="655" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">08</text>
    <text x="96" y="644" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">applyCopay(base, rule.copay_cents)</text>
    <text x="96" y="662" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">subtract fixed copay</text>
    <rect x="560" y="636" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#e0e7ff"/>
    <text x="710" y="655" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#3730a3" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">+ COPAY_APPLIED ($20)</text>

    <!-- 09 -->
    <rect x="40" y="682" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#14b8a6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="68" cy="706" r="16" fill="#14b8a6"/>
    <text x="68" y="711" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">09</text>
    <text x="96" y="700" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">applyCoinsurance(base, rule.coinsurance_pct)</text>
    <text x="96" y="718" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">subtract percentage of the remainder</text>
    <rect x="560" y="692" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#e0e7ff"/>
    <text x="710" y="711" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#3730a3" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">+ COINSURANCE_APPLIED ($26)</text>

    <!-- 10 -->
    <rect x="40" y="738" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#14b8a6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="68" cy="762" r="16" fill="#14b8a6"/>
    <text x="68" y="767" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">10</text>
    <text x="96" y="756" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">capAtOopMax(policy.oop_max, accumulator.member_oop)</text>
    <text x="96" y="774" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">if member would exceed OOP max, insurer pays 100% from here</text>
    <rect x="560" y="748" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#e0e7ff"/>
    <text x="710" y="767" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#3730a3" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">+ OOP_MAX_REACHED (if applicable)</text>

    <!-- 11 -->
    <rect x="40" y="794" width="500" height="48" rx="6" fill="#fff" stroke="#14b8a6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="68" cy="818" r="16" fill="#14b8a6"/>
    <text x="68" y="823" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">11</text>
    <text x="96" y="812" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="#1e293b">writeAccumulator(accumulator, payable)</text>
    <text x="96" y="830" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">single atomic write — same step every time</text>
    <rect x="560" y="804" width="300" height="28" rx="6" fill="#ccfbf1"/>
    <text x="710" y="823" font-size="11" font-weight="600" fill="#0f766e" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace">= APPROVED · payable=$104</text>
  </g>

  <!-- footer -->
  <text x="40" y="876" font-size="12" fill="#64748b" font-style="italic">Steps 1-5 can short-circuit. Steps 6-11 are arithmetic. Each step is a pure function. Each reason code has one test.</text>
</svg>

content.content:
1	<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 900 820" font-family="-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif">
2	  <defs>
3	    <marker id="ar4" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="8" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto">
4	      <path d="M2 1L8 5L2 9" fill="none" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
5	    </marker>
6	  </defs>
7	
8	  <rect width="900" height="820" fill="#fafafa"/>
9	
10	  <text x="40" y="46" font-size="24" font-weight="700" fill="#1e293b">04 · State machines & aggregation</text>
11	  <text x="40" y="72" font-size="14" fill="#64748b">Two state machines, one aggregation function. The claim state is derived, never set directly.</text>
12	
13	  <!-- ===== LINE ITEM STATE MACHINE ===== -->
14	  <text x="40" y="120" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#3730a3">LineItem state machine</text>
15	  <text x="40" y="140" font-size="12" fill="#64748b">Owned by the adjudication engine. Each line item moves independently.</text>
16	
17	  <g>
18	    <!-- PENDING -->
19	    <ellipse cx="120" cy="220" rx="60" ry="30" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
20	    <text x="120" y="225" font-size="13" font-weight="700" fill="#92400e" text-anchor="middle">PENDING</text>
21	
22	    <!-- APPROVED -->
23	    <ellipse cx="380" cy="170" rx="60" ry="30" fill="#ccfbf1" stroke="#14b8a6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
24	    <text x="380" y="175" font-size="13" font-weight="700" fill="#0f766e" text-anchor="middle">APPROVED</text>
25	
26	    <!-- DENIED -->
27	    <ellipse cx="380" cy="220" rx="60" ry="30" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/>
28	    <text x="380" y="225" font-size="13" font-weight="700" fill="#991b1b" text-anchor="middle">DENIED</text>
29	
30	    <!-- NEEDS_REVIEW -->
31	    <ellipse cx="380" cy="270" rx="68" ry="30" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
32	    <text x="380" y="275" font-size="13" font-weight="700" fill="#92400e" text-anchor="middle">NEEDS_REVIEW</text>
33	
34	    <!-- PAID -->
35	    <ellipse cx="640" cy="195" rx="50" ry="30" fill="#e0e7ff" stroke="#6366f1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
36	    <text x="640" y="200" font-size="13" font-weight="700" fill="#3730a3" text-anchor="middle">PAID</text>
37	
38	    <!-- transitions from PENDING -->
39	    <line x1="180" y1="210" x2="320" y2="175" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#ar4)"/>
40	    <text x="240" y="180" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">adjudicate · ok</text>
41	
42	    <line x1="180" y1="220" x2="320" y2="220" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#ar4)"/>
43	    <text x="240" y="215" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">adjudicate · deny</text>
44	
45	    <line x1="180" y1="230" x2="312" y2="263" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#ar4)"/>
46	    <text x="240" y="260" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">prior auth missing</text>
47	
48	    <!-- to PAID -->
49	    <line x1="440" y1="170" x2="592" y2="190" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#ar4)"/>
50	    <text x="510" y="175" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">recordPayment</text>
51	
52	    <!-- NEEDS_REVIEW back -->
53	    <path d="M 320 285 Q 280 320 320 280" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5" fill="none" marker-end="url(#ar4)" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/>
54	    <text x="460" y="335" font-size="10" fill="#991b1b" font-style="italic">dispute reopens DENIED → NEEDS_REVIEW · re-adjudicates</text>
55	    <path d="M 380 250 Q 460 320 380 250" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5" fill="none" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/>
56	  </g>
57	
58	  <line x1="40" y1="380" x2="860" y2="380" stroke="#cbd5e1"/>
59	
60	  <!-- ===== CLAIM STATE MACHINE ===== -->
61	  <text x="40" y="416" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#3730a3">Claim state machine</text>
62	  <text x="40" y="436" font-size="12" fill="#64748b">Derived from line-item states. Claim state is computed by claimStateFrom(lineItems), never set directly.</text>
63	
64	  <g>
65	    <ellipse cx="100" cy="510" rx="60" ry="28" fill="#e2e8f0" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5"/>
66	    <text x="100" y="515" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#475569" text-anchor="middle">SUBMITTED</text>
67	
68	    <ellipse cx="280" cy="510" rx="70" ry="28" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
69	    <text x="280" y="515" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#92400e" text-anchor="middle">UNDER_REVIEW</text>
70	
71	    <ellipse cx="480" cy="470" rx="60" ry="28" fill="#ccfbf1" stroke="#14b8a6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
72	    <text x="480" y="475" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#0f766e" text-anchor="middle">APPROVED</text>
73	
74	    <ellipse cx="480" cy="510" rx="60" ry="28" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/>
75	    <text x="480" y="515" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#991b1b" text-anchor="middle">DENIED</text>
76	
77	    <ellipse cx="480" cy="552" rx="78" ry="28" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
78	    <text x="480" y="557" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#92400e" text-anchor="middle">PARTIALLY_APPR.</text>
79	
80	    <ellipse cx="700" cy="510" rx="50" ry="28" fill="#e0e7ff" stroke="#6366f1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
81	    <text x="700" y="515" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#3730a3" text-anchor="middle">PAID</text>
82	
83	    <!-- transitions -->
84	    <line x1="160" y1="510" x2="210" y2="510" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#ar4)"/>
85	    <text x="185" y="503" font-size="10" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">begin</text>
86	
87	    <line x1="350" y1="500" x2="420" y2="475" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#ar4)"/>
88	    <line x1="350" y1="510" x2="420" y2="510" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#ar4)"/>
89	    <line x1="350" y1="520" x2="402" y2="548" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#ar4)"/>
90	    <text x="370" y="468" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">aggregate</text>
91	
92	    <line x1="540" y1="475" x2="652" y2="500" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#ar4)"/>
93	    <line x1="540" y1="510" x2="650" y2="510" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#ar4)"/>
94	    <line x1="558" y1="548" x2="660" y2="525" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#ar4)"/>
95	    <text x="600" y="495" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">recordPayment</text>

content.text:
All four read. Honest reverify: **most is locked — but your infographics encode an *earlier* model in three spots, and they surface two genuine improvements.** This is exactly why reverify matters. Keeping it simple. 🔍

## ✅ Locked & consistent with your infographics
- **Policy** — `period{start,end}`, `deductible_cents`, `oop_max_cents`, composes its rules ✓
- **Claim envelope** — `id`, `member_id`, `lines[]`, `state (derived, never set)` ✓
- **Two state machines + aggregation** — line→claim rollup; *"any NEEDS_REVIEW overrides"* ✓
- **Money flow** — plan → member; EOB is the member-facing notice ✓

## 🔄 Stale art — these boxes show the model we *replaced* (update, not re-decide)
| Infographic | Shows (old) | Locked (correct) |
|---|---|---|
| **02 · CoverageRule** | `copay_cents + coinsurance_pct + limit_per_period_cents` | `cost_share: full\|copay\|coinsurance` (one mechanism) · `limit:{unit: none\|dollars\|visits}` · `excluded` · `applies_deductible` |
| **01 · money-flow** | PHYSIO: $50 ded **+** $20 copay **+** $26 coins, **$1,500 *dollar* limit** | **PT = $40 copay, deductible waived, 20-*visit* limit.** ($1,500 dollar-limit is *chiropractic*.) |
| **03 · pipeline** | sequential deductible→copay→coinsurance | mechanism-aware **switch** on cost-share type |

These three need regenerating to match the locked model — otherwise we'd build the rejected version.

## 🟡 Your infographics surface 3 real choices — quick confirms
1. **`NEEDS_REVIEW` line state** (your 04 adds it). The brief literally says *"1 needs review"* — so **missing prior-auth → `NEEDS_REVIEW`** (a pending state), *not* an auto-denial. **Recommend YES** — matches the brief, richer state management. *(This revises our earlier "prior-auth = clean denial.")*
2. **`dx_code` + `provider` on the Claim** (your 02 includes them). This **answers last turn's doubt** → capture them as **encrypted, non-adjudicated PHI.** Confirm?
3. **`units` on LineItem · `reasons[]` array · one `service_date` per claim** (all in your art). Lean: keep all three (`units` default 1, `reasons[]` = richer EOB, claim-level date for v1 simplicity). OK?

## 🧩 Claim-process workflow — nodes & dependency (simplified)

```
INTAKE (C2)  ← YOU ARE HERE
  N1 receive POST /claims
  N2 structural validation   (fields, ≥1 line, integer cents, real date)
  N3 member resolution       (exists? → else 400)        ⟸ needs C1
  N4 compute dup fingerprint  (member+service+date+billed)
  N5 persist Claim+Lines = SUBMITTED
        │
ADJUDICATE (C3, per line)
  N6 policy active? → N7 covered/excluded? → N8 prior-auth → N9 limit
  N10 cost-share split (full|copay|coinsurance + deductible + OOP)
  N11 accumulator writeback (atomic) · N12 dup soft-flag
        │
AGGREGATE (C4)  N13 line→claim rollup
EXPLAIN  (C5)   N14 build EOB (reason codes + breakdown)
RESPOND         N15 return claim + per-line results
SETTLE   (C4)   N16 settle → PAID (reimbursement)
DISPUTE  (C6)   N17 reopen → re-adjudicate → preserve original
```

**Dependency lock:** `C1 → C2 → C3 → C4 → C5 → C6`; **C7** (edge cases) cross-cuts C3/C4; **C8** (interface) wraps C2–C5; **C9** (PHI, persistence, TDD) is continuous. This is a clean DAG — nothing downstream is needed to build the thing before it.

---

**To lock and move into the C2 intake build:** confirm the **🟡 three choices** (esp. **#1 NEEDS_REVIEW** — it's the one that changes the model). On your nod I'll: update **PRD/TRACK/domain-model**, and **regenerate the 3 stale infographics**. Then we detail the **N1–N5 intake nodes** for planning. 

Which of the three do you want to push back on? 🎯

Base directory for this skill: /Users/raunaksingh/.claude/skills/grill-me

Interview me relentlessly about every aspect of this plan until we reach a shared understanding. Walk down each branch of the design tree, resolving dependencies between decisions one-by-one. For each question, provide your recommended answer.

Ask the questions one at a time.

If a question can be answered by exploring the codebase, explore the codebase instead.


ARGUMENTS: /to-prd 

let lock the details. regarding 1st step in the workflow, 

INTAKE (C2)  ← YOU ARE HERE
  N1 receive POST /claims
  N2 structural validation   (fields, ≥1 line, integer cents, real date)
  N3 member resolution       (exists? → else 400)        ⟸ needs C1
  N4 compute dup fingerprint  (member+service+date+billed)
  N5 persist Claim+Lines = SUBMITTED

Locking C2 intake, heading toward a `/to-prd` once we've nailed it. I'll grill one decision at a time, recommendation-first, walking the N1→N5 tree. The codebase has no intake code yet (`app/src` is empty), so these are genuine design calls, not lookups.

The C2 decision tree I'll walk: **① request shape → ② validation error model → ③ `billed_cents` rule → ④ `service_date` rule → ⑤ member-resolution scope → ⑥ fingerprint → ⑦ persistence + initial states.** Starting at the root.

---

## 🎯 Q1 — The `POST /claims` request shape (the root; resolves last turn's 🟡)

Everything in N2–N5 validates *against* this shape, so we lock it first. My recommendation, folding in what your infographics already decided:

```jsonc
POST /claims
{
  "member_id": "M001",              // ✅ required — the only identity field
  "service_date": "2026-03-14",     // ✅ required — CLAIM-level (your 02 art), ISO, not future
  "provider": "Dr. Smith / NPI…",   // 🔵 optional — captured PHI, NOT adjudicated (your 02)
  "diagnosis_code": "M54.5",        // 🔵 optional — captured PHI, NOT adjudicated (your 02)
  "line_items": [                   // ✅ required, ≥1
    {
      "service_code": "PHYSICAL_THERAPY", // ✅ required
      "billed_cents": 20000,              // ✅ required, positive integer
      "units": 1,                         // 🔵 optional, default 1 (your art)
      "prior_auth_present": false         // 🔵 optional, default false — per LINE
    }
  ]
}
```

**The embedded calls I'm recommending:**
- **`service_date` at the claim level, not per line** — your 02 art puts it there; simpler for v1 (one date per claim). Trade-off: can't model a claim spanning multiple service dates, which I think is fine to skip and document.
- **`provider` + `diagnosis_code` at claim level, optional, captured-but-not-adjudicated PHI** — this is where we *show* the sensitive-data handling the brief asks for. (Confirms your 🟡 #2.)
- **`prior_auth_present` per line** (not claim) — prior-auth is per-service, and your 03 pipeline gates on it per line.
- **`units` per line, default 1.**

**Where I need you:** Two things I'm genuinely unsure you'll agree on —
1. **`service_date`: claim-level (my rec) or per-line?** Per-line is more correct (different services on different days) but adds a field to every line and a per-line policy-active check. For a take-home, is the simpler claim-level date acceptable to you, or does per-line matter?
2. **`prior_auth_present` as a plain boolean** — or do you (per the 03 pipeline's `prior_auth_id`) want an actual auth *reference/number*? The boolean is enough for v1 math; the id is more realistic but unused. Which?

Pick those two and the shape locks — then Q2 (how we report validation errors). 🚦

content.text:
Good redirect — let's stay at the domain/deliverables level, not the API. Three things: clear auth, confirm the LineItem, then lay out the workflow sections to select. 🎯

## 🔐 Auth — cleared, and parked

The key distinction:

- **Domain auth** — user registration, login, RBAC, multi-role access → **explicitly out of scope** (brief). We build *none* of it.
- **Transport guard** — a single static key in a header just to hit the demo endpoints → this is **not** the domain auth the brief excludes; it's a thin gate on the demo surface, zero domain value.

**Verdict: allowed but optional, and parked.** It's a C8 (interface) detail — decided at the very end, if at all. Your instinct (static key, header) is fine and won't violate the brief. Not deciding now. ✅

## 📦 LineItem — confirm the contents (domain, not API)

The LineItem is the **unit of adjudication**. Proposed locked contents:

| Field | Role | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `id` · `claim_id` | identity + parent | 🔒 |
| `service_code` | selects the coverage rule (unknown → `NO_COVERAGE`) | 🔒 |
| `billed_cents` | provider charge, positive integer (allowed==billed v1) | 🔒 |
| `units` | quantity, default 1 | 🔒 (your art) |
| `prior_auth_present` | per-line gate | 🔒 (your art) |
| `status` | `PENDING → APPROVED/DENIED/NEEDS_REVIEW → PAID` | 🔒 (your 04) |
| `fingerprint` | dup detection, computed at intake | 🔒 |
| **`service_date`** | when the service happened | **⬅ one open call** |

**Deliberately NOT on the line:** `diagnosis_code` + `provider` live on the **Claim** (claim-level PHI, your 02); no ICD/modifier/place-of-service/NPI per line (out of scope — `service_code` encodes the setting).

**The one open call:** `service_date` on the **Claim** (one date for the whole claim) or on each **LineItem**? → **I recommend claim-level for v1** (simpler; document the limitation). Confirm or override.

## 🗂️ Claims workflow — the sections to select

Here's the full claim workflow as selectable deliverable sections. Pick which belong in **this** slice (my rec: lock the **intake slice** now, defer adjudication+):

| § | Section | C-class | In this slice? |
|---|---|---|---|
| A | **Claim + LineItem entities** (+ relationship, PHI fields) | domain | ✅ yes — foundation |
| B | **Structural validation** (fields, ≥1 line, integer cents, real date) | C2 | ✅ yes |
| C | **Member resolution** (exists → else reject) | C2 | ✅ yes |
| D | **Duplicate fingerprint** (compute at intake) | C2 | ✅ yes |
| E | **Persistence** (Claim+Lines = SUBMITTED) | C2 | ✅ yes |
| F | Adjudication per line | C3 | ⏸️ defer |
| G | State machines (line + claim) | C4 | ⏸️ defer (define, build later) |
| H | Aggregation (line→claim) | C4 | ⏸️ defer |
| I | Explanation / EOB | C5 | ⏸️ defer |
| J | Dispute / re-adjudication | C6 | ⏸️ defer |
| K | Settle / PAID | C4 | ⏸️ defer |
| L | PHI handling · persistence · TDD | C9 | 🔄 continuous |

---

**Two confirms and we've got a clean intake deliverable to take to `/to-prd`:**
1. **LineItem** — accept the contents above, and `service_date` at **claim-level** (or do you want per-line)?
2. **The slice** — lock **A–E (intake)** as this workflow's deliverable, adjudication+ deferred? Or do you want a different cut?

content.text:
**LineItem is an Artifact** — a *noun*, not an actor and not an action. Quick placement in our framework:

| Category | Grammar | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| **Actor** | the *subject* (initiates) | Member, System/Adjudicator |
| **Action** | the *verb* (a step done) | submit, adjudicate, settle, dispute |
| **Artifact** | the *object* (data acted upon) | Policy, Claim, **LineItem**, Adjudication, Accumulator |

## Definition

> A **LineItem** is one service line inside a Claim — the **unit of adjudication**. The Claim is the *envelope*; the LineItem is *one charge* in it. Think itemized bill: the bill is the Claim, each row is a LineItem.

**Relationship:** `Claim 1 ──N LineItem` (a claim has one or more line items).

**Grammatically:** it's the *direct object* of the adjudicate action — *"the System **adjudicates** (action) each **LineItem** (artifact) on the **Claim** (artifact)."* It never *does* anything; things are *done to* it.

## Where it sits in the flow

```
SUBMIT (action, by Member)
   └─ creates → Claim (artifact)  ──contains──>  LineItem[] (artifact)   ← born here, state = PENDING
                                                      │
ADJUDICATE (action, by System) ─── acts on ──────────┘   ← the unit each decision is made about
                                                      │
                                                 produces → Adjudication (artifact, 1 per line)
                                                      │
                                                 LineItem state → APPROVED / DENIED / NEEDS_REVIEW
                                                      │
AGGREGATE (action) ── rolls LineItem states up ──> Claim.status (derived)
```

**Why this matters for us:** because the LineItem is an artifact *with its own lifecycle*, we get **two state machines** — the LineItem decides independently, and the Claim's status is *derived* by aggregating its LineItems. That's the whole reason "3 covered / 1 denied / 1 needs review" → claim `PARTIALLY_APPROVED`. The LineItem is where adjudication actually happens; the Claim just bundles and rolls up.

So in N1–N5 intake: the LineItem is **created and persisted** (as a noun, state `PENDING`); it isn't *acted on* by adjudication until C3. 

Does that placement match your mental model — LineItem as the granular artifact the engine decides on, nested under the Claim envelope?

content.text:
A line item's `service_code` is drawn from our **closed service catalog** — the same 12 services our coverage rules cover (from C1). So "all the line items we can have on a claim" = these 12 service types. A claim carries one or more of them (repeats allowed — e.g. two `PHYSICAL_THERAPY` lines).

| # | `service_code` | Service | Cost-share | Limit | Prior auth |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `PREVENTIVE` | Annual physical / preventive | full coverage | — | no |
| 2 | `PCP_VISIT` | Primary care visit | $25 copay | — | no |
| 3 | `SPECIALIST_VISIT` | Specialist visit | $50 copay | — | no |
| 4 | `URGENT_CARE` | Urgent care | $50 copay | — | no |
| 5 | `EMERGENCY_ROOM` | Emergency room | $300 copay | — | no |
| 6 | `LAB` | Lab / X-ray | 20% coinsurance | — | no |
| 7 | `MRI` | MRI / advanced imaging | 20% coinsurance | — | **yes** |
| 8 | `OUTPATIENT_SURGERY` | Outpatient surgery | 20% coinsurance | — | **yes** |
| 9 | `INPATIENT_HOSPITAL` | Inpatient hospital | 20% coinsurance | — | **yes** |
| 10 | `PHYSICAL_THERAPY` | Physical therapy | $40 copay | **20 visits/yr** | no |
| 11 | `CHIROPRACTIC` | Chiropractic | $25 copay | **$1,500/yr** | no |
| 12 | `ADULT_DENTAL` | Adult dental | — | — | — (**excluded**) |

**Notes:**
- This is the **closed catalog**. A line item with any `service_code` **not** in this list → `NO_COVERAGE` at adjudication (it's accepted at intake, denied at C3 — never an intake reject).
- The catalog deliberately spans every adjudication branch: full-coverage / copay / coinsurance / visit-limit / dollar-limit / prior-auth / excluded.
- These 12 are **per-policy coverage rules** — each policy seeds its own set. A line item references one by `service_code`; the matching rule decides coverage.

So: a **LineItem** = `{ service_code (one of these 12), billed_cents, units, prior_auth_present }` + system fields. The 12 are the *vocabulary*; a claim is any combination of them.

Want me to lock this catalog (the `service_code` constants above) into the PRD + domain-model as the official C1 catalog, so C2 intake has a fixed vocabulary to resolve against?

| Artifact | Final lock | 
|---|---|

| Service catalog | 🔒 the **12 `service_code`s** (closed; unlisted → `NO_COVERAGE`) |

| Accumulator | 🔒 `deductible_met` + `oop_met` + per-service `limit_used` |

**Genuinely still open (don't block — adjudication-side, deferred):** **Q4** dispute-resolution policy · **Q6 (new)** how `NEEDS_REVIEW` resolves in v1 (lean: member re-submits with auth). Plus **3 stale infographics** to regenerate (CoverageRule, money-flow, pipeline) to match the union/unit model.

# TRACK.md — live cross-session memory

The only file that mutates freely. Every agent updates it at session close. Keep it
honest and current — the next agent trusts it instead of re-deriving everything.

## Current focus

Phase 01 framing — coverage/policy model **locked**; payment direction (reimbursement,
plan → member) and the **Member actor** confirmed. Walking the Actors · Actions · Artifacts
map: Policy ✅ · CoverageRule ✅ · Member ✅ → **Claim next**. Then resolve Q4/Q5 and close
framing.

## Current phase

`01-framing`

## Open questions

- [x] **Q1 — Prior auth modeling.** RESOLVED → boolean precondition; missing → `PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED`, payable 0. PPO reduce-to-50% penalty documented as a divergence, not built.
- [x] **Q2 — Out-of-network policy.** RESOLVED → in-network only for v1 (allowed == billed); OON/unlisted service → `NO_COVERAGE`. Network/metal-tier/family fields omitted (no math impact).
- [x] **Q3 — Accumulator period boundaries.** RESOLVED → fixed plan-year window keyed on the policy.
- [ ] **Q4 — Dispute resolution.** Auto re-adjudicate vs. reviewer queue. Leaning auto, original preserved immutably.
- [ ] **Q5 — Duplicate handling.** Hard reject vs. soft duplicate flag. Leaning soft flag, `DUPLICATE_LINE_ITEM`.

## Blocked

Nothing blocked.

## Decisions log

| # | Date | Decision | Reason | Reversed? |
|---|------|----------|--------|-----------|
| 1 | 2026-06-18 | TypeScript (strict) + Node 20 + vitest | Static types encode the domain and catch money/null bugs at compile time; vitest is fast and test-first friendly. | No |
| 2 | 2026-06-18 | SQLite over Postgres | Reviewer clones and runs with zero DB setup; synchronous better-sqlite3 keeps adjudication deterministic. Postgres adds clone friction for no rubric gain. | No |
| 3 | 2026-06-18 | Typed config over a rules DSL | 80/20 call for a 48h budget: typed coverage-rule records are reviewable and type-checked; a DSL/engine is meta-machinery the assignment explicitly does not reward. | No |
| 4 | 2026-06-18 | Integer cents for all money | Float math on money is the single most common claims bug (coinsurance %, accumulation). Integers make math exact and tests trustworthy. | No |
| 5 | 2026-06-18 | Cost-share as a discriminated union (`full_coverage`\|`copay`\|`coinsurance`) | Real benefits (UHC/Aetna/Cigna/BCBS/ACA) use exactly one mechanism per service; union makes the model say it and the adjudicator an exhaustive switch. | No |
| 6 | 2026-06-18 | Unit-typed limits (`none`\|`dollars`\|`visits`) | The most common real limit is a visit/day cap, which a dollars-only field can't express; dollars still satisfies the brief's "$Y/yr". | No |
| 7 | 2026-06-18 | Prior-auth = clean denial; OON/network/metal/family omitted | Each stored field must trace to a real adjudication effect in a single-network, per-member, allowed==billed v1. | No |
| 8 | 2026-06-18 | Reimbursement model (plan → member); PAID via explicit settle action, gateway success assumed | Brief says "claims for reimbursement" and lists `paid` in the lifecycle. No real payment processing in scope → record the transition. Likely a 5th interface action. | No |
| 9 | 2026-06-18 | Member = opaque `member_id` anchor; PII minimized/separated, encryption-at-rest candidate | Brief flags sensitive health data; engine adjudicates on `member_id → policy + accumulators` and never needs the name. One human persona (no auth/roles). | No |

## Domain research findings (2026-06-18)

Source: 6-agent web research across UnitedHealthcare, Aetna, Cigna, BCBS SBCs + ACA/CMS;
synthesis in `ai-artifacts/02-domain-research/`. Key findings that shaped the model:

- **Three cost-share modes, never a stack:** preventive = free (ACA, no deductible); office/
  specialist/urgent/ER = flat **copay** (deductible waived); imaging/surgery/hospital =
  **coinsurance after deductible**. ER's copay-then-coinsurance is the one common exception.
- **Visit/day limits dominate** (PT ~20–60/yr, home health ~60–120/yr, SNF ~25–180 days/yr);
  **dollar limits are rare** (chiro ~$1,500/yr, hearing aids, infertility). → limits need a unit.
- **Copay waives deductible but counts to OOP; coinsurance applies deductible first.** Kept as an
  explicit per-rule `applies_deductible` bool (exceptions exist) rather than derived.
- **Two "no"s:** explicit `excluded` (adult dental/vision) vs. no rule at all → `NO_COVERAGE`.
- **Dropped as no-math-impact in v1:** metal tier, HMO/PPO network, OON tier, family deductible,
  separate Rx deductible, referral gating.

## Session log

| Date | Agent | Phase | Outcome | Next |
|------|-------|-------|---------|------|
| 2026-06-18 | Claude Code (Opus) | 01-framing | Scaffold created and committed; framing conversation in progress. | Resolve Q1–Q3, log framing decisions, move to 02 domain research. |
| 2026-06-18 | Claude Code (Opus) | 01-framing → 02-domain-research | Ran real-insurer coverage research; locked cost-share union + unit-typed limits + 12-rule seed set; resolved Q1–Q3; propagated to PRD, insurance-domain skill, docs/, and artifacts. | Resolve Q4 (dispute) + Q5 (duplicate), then close framing → design. |

## Notes for next agent

- Scaffold lives at the repo root (`/insurance`), not in a nested `claims-system/` — the
  existing `.git` is at the root, so the root *is* the project.
- `project-docs/` holds the original assignment brief. Reference it; do not edit it.
- Nothing in `app/` yet — first code must arrive via a `/tdd-cycle`, test first.
- When you start phase 02, load the `insurance-domain` skill before naming anything.

# TRACK.md — live cross-session memory

The only file that mutates freely. Every agent updates it at session close. Keep it
honest and current — the next agent trusts it instead of re-deriving everything.

## Current focus

Phase 01 framing — **all artifacts + actors locked** (Policy, CoverageRule, 12-service
catalog, Member, Claim, LineItem, Adjudication, Accumulator, Dispute; actors: Member,
System/Adjudicator, Insurer). **C2 claim-intake slice (N1–N5) defined.** Next: `/to-prd`
the intake slice and plan the build. Deferred: Q4 dispute-resolution policy, Q6 NEEDS_REVIEW
resolution path. Regenerate the 3 stale infographics (CoverageRule, money-flow, pipeline) to
match the cost-share-union + unit-typed-limit model.

## Current phase

`01-framing`

## Open questions

- [x] **Q1 — Prior auth modeling.** RESOLVED → boolean precondition; missing → `PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED`, payable 0. PPO reduce-to-50% penalty documented as a divergence, not built.
- [x] **Q2 — Out-of-network policy.** RESOLVED → in-network only for v1 (allowed == billed); OON/unlisted service → `NO_COVERAGE`. Network/metal-tier/family fields omitted (no math impact).
- [x] **Q3 — Accumulator period boundaries.** RESOLVED → fixed plan-year window keyed on the policy.
- [x] **Q5 — Duplicate handling.** RESOLVED → fingerprint (`member_id + service_code + service_date + billed_cents`) computed at **intake**; the duplicate *decision* is made at **adjudication** as a soft `DUPLICATE_LINE_ITEM` (payable 0). Not an intake reject.
- [ ] **Q4 — Dispute resolution.** Auto re-adjudicate vs. reviewer queue. Leaning auto, original preserved immutably. (C6, deferred.)
- [ ] **Q6 — NEEDS_REVIEW resolution path.** With no human reviewer in scope, how does a `NEEDS_REVIEW` line (prior-auth missing) resolve? Leaning: member re-submits with `prior_auth_present=true`; manual review out of scope. (C3/C6, deferred.)

## Blocked

Nothing blocked.

## Decisions log

| # | Date | Decision | Reason | Reversed? |
|---|------|----------|--------|-----------|
| 1 | 2026-06-18 | TypeScript (strict) + Node 20 + vitest | Static types encode the domain and catch money/null bugs at compile time; vitest is fast and test-first friendly. | No |
| 2 | 2026-06-18 | SQLite over Postgres | Reviewer clones and runs with zero DB setup; synchronous better-sqlite3 keeps adjudication deterministic. Postgres adds clone friction for no rubric gain. | No |
| 3 | 2026-06-18 | Typed config over a rules DSL | 80/20 call for a 48h budget: typed coverage-rule records are reviewable and type-checked; a DSL/engine is meta-machinery the assignment explicitly does not reward. | No |
| 4 | 2026-06-18 | Integer cents for all money | Float math on money is the single most common claims bug (coinsurance %, accumulation). Integers make math exact and tests trustworthy. | No |
| 5 | 2026-06-18 | Cost-share as a discriminated union (`full_coverage`\|`copay`\|`coinsurance`) | Real benefits (UHC/Aetna/Cigna/BCBS/ACA) use exactly one mechanism per service; union makes the model say it and the adjudicator an exhaustive switch. | No |
| 6 | 2026-06-18 | Unit-typed limits (`none`\|`dollars`\|`visits`) | The most common real limit is a visit/day cap, which a dollars-only field can't express; dollars still satisfies the brief's "$Y/yr". | No |
| 7 | 2026-06-18 | Prior-auth = clean denial; OON/network/metal/family omitted | Each stored field must trace to a real adjudication effect in a single-network, per-member, allowed==billed v1. | No |
| 8 | 2026-06-18 | Reimbursement model (plan → member); PAID via explicit settle action, gateway success assumed | Brief says "claims for reimbursement" and lists `paid` in the lifecycle. No real payment processing in scope → record the transition. Likely a 5th interface action. | No |
| 9 | 2026-06-18 | Member = opaque `member_id` anchor; PII minimized/separated, encryption-at-rest candidate | Brief flags sensitive health data; engine adjudicates on `member_id → policy + accumulators` and never needs the name. One human persona (no auth/roles). | No |

## Domain research findings (2026-06-18)

Source: 6-agent web research across UnitedHealthcare, Aetna, Cigna, BCBS SBCs + ACA/CMS;
synthesis in `ai-artifacts/02-domain-research/`. Key findings that shaped the model:

- **Three cost-share modes, never a stack:** preventive = free (ACA, no deductible); office/
  specialist/urgent/ER = flat **copay** (deductible waived); imaging/surgery/hospital =
  **coinsurance after deductible**. ER's copay-then-coinsurance is the one common exception.
- **Visit/day limits dominate** (PT ~20–60/yr, home health ~60–120/yr, SNF ~25–180 days/yr);
  **dollar limits are rare** (chiro ~$1,500/yr, hearing aids, infertility). → limits need a unit.
- **Copay waives deductible but counts to OOP; coinsurance applies deductible first.** Kept as an
  explicit per-rule `applies_deductible` bool (exceptions exist) rather than derived.
- **Two "no"s:** explicit `excluded` (adult dental/vision) vs. no rule at all → `NO_COVERAGE`.
- **Dropped as no-math-impact in v1:** metal tier, HMO/PPO network, OON tier, family deductible,
  separate Rx deductible, referral gating.

## Session log

| Date | Agent | Phase | Outcome | Next |
|------|-------|-------|---------|------|
| 2026-06-18 | Claude Code (Opus) | 01-framing | Scaffold created and committed; framing conversation in progress. | Resolve Q1–Q3, log framing decisions, move to 02 domain research. |
| 2026-06-18 | Claude Code (Opus) | 01-framing → 02-domain-research | Ran real-insurer coverage research; locked cost-share union + unit-typed limits + 12-rule seed set; resolved Q1–Q3; propagated to PRD, insurance-domain skill, docs/, and artifacts. | Resolve Q4 (dispute) + Q5 (duplicate), then close framing → design. |

## Notes for next agent

- Scaffold lives at the repo root (`/insurance`), not in a nested `claims-system/` — the
  existing `.git` is at the root, so the root *is* the project.
- `project-docs/` holds the original assignment brief. Reference it; do not edit it.
- Nothing in `app/` yet — first code must arrive via a `/tdd-cycle`, test first.
- When you start phase 02, load the `insurance-domain` skill before naming anything.

content.text: Now the decisions log.

input.old_string:
**Reversible?:** Moderate — touches the accumulator's limit column and the limit step.

---

## Decisions resolved this framing session
input.new_string:
**Reversible?:** Moderate — touches the accumulator's limit column and the limit step.

### 8 — Prior-auth missing → `NEEDS_REVIEW`, not an auto-denial

**Chose:** A line that requires prior auth and lacks it goes to a `NEEDS_REVIEW` line-item state; the claim aggregates to `UNDER_REVIEW` while any line is in review.
**Over:** Treating missing prior auth as a clean `DENIED` (the earlier decision #7 stance).
**Trade-off:** The brief explicitly lists *"1 needs review"* as a line outcome, and a review
state is richer, more honest state management than collapsing it into a denial. Cost: a v1
with no human-reviewer queue needs a defined resolution path (Q6) — leaning "member
re-submits with `prior_auth_present=true`."
**Reversible?:** Yes — it's a state-machine + reason-mapping change. **Revises #7.**

### 9 — Capture `diagnosis_code` + `provider` as encrypted, non-adjudicated PHI

**Chose:** Capture `diagnosis_code` and `provider` on the Claim, store them as sensitive
(encryption-at-rest candidate), and **never** read them in adjudication.
**Over:** Omitting them entirely (the earlier research lean).
**Trade-off:** The brief explicitly names them as sensitive health data; capturing them is
where we *demonstrate* the PHI-handling the brief is testing. Cost: PHI we must protect for
zero math benefit — accepted deliberately, documented as the showcase of the PHI stance.
**Reversible?:** Yes — they're inert fields.

### 10 — Claim + LineItem shape, closed 12-code catalog, `reasons[]` array

**Chose:** Claim = `{ id, member_id, service_date (claim-level), provider, diagnosis_code,
status (derived) }`; LineItem = `{ id, claim_id, service_code, billed_cents, units,
prior_auth_present, status, fingerprint }`; a **closed 12-entry `service_code` catalog**;
Adjudication carries a `reasons[]` array.
**Over:** Per-line service dates; an open service vocabulary; a single dominant reason code.
**Trade-off:** Claim-level date + closed catalog keep v1 simple and make `NO_COVERAGE`
well-defined. `reasons[]` gives a richer EOB breakdown than one dominant code. Cost: can't
model a claim spanning multiple service dates (documented).
**Reversible?:** Moderate for the date; easy for the rest.

### 11 — C2 intake: reject = HTTP 4xx, never persisted (no `REJECTED` state)

**Chose:** Structural/identity failures (bad shape, non-integer cents, future/invalid date,
**unknown member**) → HTTP `4xx` with `{ errors: [{ field, code, message }] }`, nothing
persisted. Member *existence* is an intake reject; policy *active-on-date* is an
adjudication `POLICY_NOT_ACTIVE` deny.
**Over:** Persisting a `REJECTED` claim row for auditability.
**Trade-off:** Matches the industry reject≠deny boundary (a reject never enters the system,
no appeal) and avoids bloating the frozen claim state machine. Cost: no built-in audit trail
of bad submissions — if needed, log them separately, not as Claim rows.
**Reversible?:** Yes — a reject log can be added without touching the claim machine.

---

## Decisions resolved this framing session

toolUseResult.newString:
**Reversible?:** Moderate — touches the accumulator's limit column and the limit step.

### 8 — Prior-auth missing → `NEEDS_REVIEW`, not an auto-denial

**Chose:** A line that requires prior auth and lacks it goes to a `NEEDS_REVIEW` line-item state; the claim aggregates to `UNDER_REVIEW` while any line is in review.
**Over:** Treating missing prior auth as a clean `DENIED` (the earlier decision #7 stance).
**Trade-off:** The brief explicitly lists *"1 needs review"* as a line outcome, and a review
state is richer, more honest state management than collapsing it into a denial. Cost: a v1
with no human-reviewer queue needs a defined resolution path (Q6) — leaning "member
re-submits with `prior_auth_present=true`."
**Reversible?:** Yes — it's a state-machine + reason-mapping change. **Revises #7.**

### 9 — Capture `diagnosis_code` + `provider` as encrypted, non-adjudicated PHI

**Chose:** Capture `diagnosis_code` and `provider` on the Claim, store them as sensitive
(encryption-at-rest candidate), and **never** read them in adjudication.
**Over:** Omitting them entirely (the earlier research lean).
**Trade-off:** The brief explicitly names them as sensitive health data; capturing them is
where we *demonstrate* the PHI-handling the brief is testing. Cost: PHI we must protect for
zero math benefit — accepted deliberately, documented as the showcase of the PHI stance.
**Reversible?:** Yes — they're inert fields.

### 10 — Claim + LineItem shape, closed 12-code catalog, `reasons[]` array

**Chose:** Claim = `{ id, member_id, service_date (claim-level), provider, diagnosis_code,
status (derived) }`; LineItem = `{ id, claim_id, service_code, billed_cents, units,
prior_auth_present, status, fingerprint }`; a **closed 12-entry `service_code` catalog**;
Adjudication carries a `reasons[]` array.
**Over:** Per-line service dates; an open service vocabulary; a single dominant reason code.
**Trade-off:** Claim-level date + closed catalog keep v1 simple and make `NO_COVERAGE`
well-defined. `reasons[]` gives a richer EOB breakdown than one dominant code. Cost: can't
model a claim spanning multiple service dates (documented).
**Reversible?:** Moderate for the date; easy for the rest.

### 11 — C2 intake: reject = HTTP 4xx, never persisted (no `REJECTED` state)

**Chose:** Structural/identity failures (bad shape, non-integer cents, future/invalid date,
**unknown member**) → HTTP `4xx` with `{ errors: [{ field, code, message }] }`, nothing
persisted. Member *existence* is an intake reject; policy *active-on-date* is an
adjudication `POLICY_NOT_ACTIVE` deny.
**Over:** Persisting a `REJECTED` claim row for auditability.
**Trade-off:** Matches the industry reject≠deny boundary (a reject never enters the system,
no appeal) and avoids bloating the frozen claim state machine. Cost: no built-in audit trail
of bad submissions — if needed, log them separately, not as Claim rows.
**Reversible?:** Yes — a reject log can be added without touching the claim machine.

---

## Decisions resolved this framing session

# Decisions & trade-offs

> Status: template, pre-seeded from framing. Add a numbered entry whenever a real choice is
> made. Each entry: the decision, the alternative not taken, and the trade-off. This is the
> doc that shows engineering judgment — keep it honest.

## Format

`#N — Decision` → **Chose:** … **Over:** … **Trade-off:** … **Reversible?:** …

---

### 1 — Language and runtime: TypeScript (strict) + Node 20, vitest

**Chose:** TypeScript strict mode on Node 20, tested with vitest.
**Over:** plain JS, or Python/FastAPI.
**Trade-off:** Strict types add upfront friction but encode the domain (money as branded
cents, exhaustive reason-code switches) and catch a class of bugs at compile time. vitest
keeps the red-green loop fast.
**Reversible?:** Low cost early, high cost later.

### 2 — Persistence: SQLite (better-sqlite3) over Postgres

**Chose:** SQLite via better-sqlite3.
**Over:** Postgres / Docker-compose stack.
**Trade-off:** A reviewer clones and runs with zero DB setup, and the synchronous driver
keeps adjudication deterministic and easy to test. Cost: no real concurrency story; fine
for a demo, called out as a limitation in self-review.
**Reversible?:** Moderate — schema is portable; the synchronous-driver assumption is not.

### 3 — Rule representation: typed config over a DSL / rules engine

**Chose:** Coverage rules as typed config records, applied by a fixed-order adjudicator.
**Over:** A rules DSL or a pluggable rules engine.
**Trade-off:** Typed config is reviewable, type-checked, and enough for the rules in scope.
A DSL is meta-machinery the assignment explicitly does not reward and would eat the 48h
budget. Cost: adding a fundamentally new *kind* of rule means code, not config.
**Reversible?:** Yes — the adjudicator is the seam; a DSL could be layered later.

### 4 — Money: integer cents everywhere

**Chose:** All monetary values as integer cents; percentages applied with explicit rounding
at the cents step.
**Over:** Floating-point dollars, or a decimal library.
**Trade-off:** Integers make money math exact and tests trustworthy — float math on money
(coinsurance %, accumulation) is the most common claims bug. Cost: must round consciously
where coinsurance produces fractional cents (member + plan shares must sum to allowed).
**Reversible?:** Hard — touches every amount; chosen deliberately up front.

### 5 — Interface: REST API (fastify) over CLI or web UI

**Chose:** Four REST endpoints via fastify.
**Over:** CLI, or a web UI.
**Trade-off:** REST is the clearest way to demonstrate the domain to a reviewer and maps
1:1 to the operations (submit, fetch, explain, dispute). A UI would burn budget on
non-rubric surface. _Confirm this stands after framing._
**Reversible?:** Yes — the domain core is interface-agnostic.

### 6 — Cost-share: a discriminated union, not nullable copay + coinsurance fields

**Chose:** `costShare: { type: "full_coverage" } | { type: "copay"; copayCents } | { type: "coinsurance"; rate }` — one mechanism per rule.
**Over:** a flat record with both `copayCents` and `coinsuranceRate` applied in sequence.
**Trade-off:** Research across UHC, Aetna, Cigna, BCBS, and ACA showed real benefits use
*one* mechanism — preventive is full coverage, office/ER/urgent are copay, imaging/surgery/
hospital are coinsurance after deductible. A union makes the model *say* that, turns the
adjudicator into an exhaustive switch, and rejects incoherent rules at the type level. Cost:
the rare copay-then-coinsurance case (ER) is approximated by its dominant component.
**Reversible?:** Yes — a 4th union variant can be added; the adjudicator switch is the seam.

### 7 — Annual limit: unit-typed (`none` | `dollars` | `visits`), not dollars-only

**Chose:** `limit: { unit: "none" } | { unit: "dollars"; amountCents } | { unit: "visits"; count }`,
with the accumulator's `limit_used` tracked in the same unit.
**Over:** a single `annualLimitCents` field.
**Trade-off:** The brief's one concrete example ("$Y per year") is a dollar limit — but the
*most common* real limit is a visit/day cap (PT 20 visits/yr, SNF 60 days/yr), which a
cents-only field literally cannot express. The unit discriminator handles both on one code
path. Cost: the accumulator and limit check become unit-aware (small). This is a deliberate
step beyond the brief's literal ask, justified by domain research.
**Reversible?:** Moderate — touches the accumulator's limit column and the limit step.

---

## Decisions resolved this framing session

- **Prior-auth modeling** (Q1) — boolean precondition; missing → `PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED`, payable 0. PPO reduce-to-50% penalty documented as a divergence, not built.
- **Out-of-network** (Q2) — in-network only for v1 (allowed == billed); OON/unlisted service → `NO_COVERAGE`. Network/metal/family fields omitted.
- **Accumulator period** (Q3) — fixed plan-year window keyed on the policy.

## Decisions still open (resolve and move up)

- **Dispute resolution** — auto re-adjudicate vs. reviewer queue (leaning auto, immutable original).
- **Duplicate handling** — hard reject vs. soft duplicate flag (leaning soft flag, `DUPLICATE_LINE_ITEM`).

## Assumptions about the domain

1. Single currency (USD), integer cents.
2. One policy per member per plan year; no coordination of benefits.
3. Allowed amount == billed amount (no fee schedule lookup).
4. Plan year is a fixed window on the policy; accumulators align to it.
5. Prior auth is a recorded precondition, not a workflow.
6. Determinism over wall-clock; concurrency beyond SQLite's single-writer is out of scope.
7. One cost-share mechanism per service; copay-then-coinsurance approximated by dominant component.
8. Limits are unit-typed (`dollars` or `visits`); supply-window/replacement-frequency limits out of scope.

_Add to this list whenever the build forces a judgment call the assignment left open._

## What's actually locked vs. still remaining

| Locked ✅ | Still remaining ⏳ (C3 adjudication) |
|---|---|
| **Artifacts** (nouns + attributes): Policy, CoverageRule, catalog, Member, Claim, LineItem, **Adjudication *record shape***, Accumulator, Dispute | The **adjudication *behavior*** — the order/flow, prior-auth routing (`NEEDS_REVIEW` vs deny), how `reasons[]` is populated, cost-share math, limit straddle, OOP cap, atomic writeback |
| **Actors**: Member, System, Insurer | **Aggregation** detail (line→claim rules) |
| **C2 intake** (N1–N5) + reject model | **Dispute** re-adjudication (Q4) · **NEEDS_REVIEW** resolution (Q6) |
